content.full_text:
Rec'd 4/1/90
3-37

DANNY WALSH
33 PAYNE RIVER CIRCLE
SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95831
(916)393-4825

April 16, 1990

Ms. Blanca Breeze
Fair Political Practices Commission
PO Box 807
Sacramento, California 95804

Dear Ms. Breeze:

It was a pleasure speaking with you the other day with respect
to my particular question. I now request written confirmation of
your oral opinion, either by an advice letter, or some other
written document.

To refresh your memory, the facts of my question are as
follows:

1. I am a former member of the State Water Resources Control
Board. My term expired in February of 1990. As a member of that
Board, I served as Chairman of the Santa Monica Bay Restoration
Project.

2. I am now a private consultant. The Air Resources Board
would like to contract with me to act as the facilitator for the
Santa Monica Bay Restoration Project.

3. The funds to pay for my services would come out of the Air
Resources Board's budget.

4. The Santa Monica Bay Restoration Project is a multi-
federal and state effort designed to assist with the clean-up of
Santa Monica Bay.

To my knowledge, the State Water Resources Control Board and
the Air Resources Board are separate "agencies." Their respective
budgets are considered separately by the legislature. My question:
am I prohibited under Government Code sections 87400-87405, or any
other relevant law from contracting with the Air Resources Board
to act as the facilitator for the Santa Monica Bay Restoration
Project? If no conflict exists, are there any restrictions
proscribed by the Political Reform Act to such a contract, as I
intend to enter into other consulting contracts. Previously you

[The image appears to be a very faint or poorly scanned document with text that is largely illegible. While there are multiple paragraphs visible, the text is too blurry and faint to accurately transcribe with confidence. The document appears to be several pages long with multiple sections of body text, but I cannot reliably make out the specific words and content to provide an accurate transcription as requested.]

Ms. Blanca Breeze
April 16, 1990
Page 2

advised me that there was no conflict.  Could you kindly provide
me with written confirmation of your advice? Thank you.

Very truly yours,
[signature]
DANNY WALSH

cc: Janann Sharpless

DECLASSIFIED Authority EO 13526 SEC 3.3(B)(1), (2)
NARA, BY Robert Bauer DATE 01/16/2017

[illegible] without further official action. Violations of the
[illegible] may result in civil and/or criminal penalties.

We believe that the new stated task we outlined
[illegible] Freedom Factor [illegible] will [illegible] the pertinent question of what am the LIVE
with the [illegible] [illegible] [illegible] [illegible]

[illegible]

[illegible] [illegible] [illegible]

[illegible] [illegible] [illegible]

[illegible] [illegible] [illegible]

This page appears to be heavily redacted or degraded, with most of the content being illegible or obscured.

embedding.qa_text: April 16, 1990 Ms. Blanca Breeze Fair Political Practices Commission PO Box 807 Sacramento, California 95804 Dear Ms. Breeze: It was a pleasure speaking with you the other day with respect to my particular question. I now request written confirmation of your oral opinion, either by an advice letter, or some other written document. To refresh your memory, the facts of my question are as follows: 1. I am a former member of the State Water Resources Control Board. My term expired in February of 1990. As a member of that Board, I served as Chairman of the Santa Monica Bay Restoration Project. 2. I am now a private consultant. The Air Resources Board would like to contract with me to act as the facilitator for the Santa Monica Bay Restoration Project. 3. The funds to pay for my services would come out of the Air Resources Board's budget. 4. The Santa Monica Bay Restoration Project is a multi-federal and state effort designed to assist with the clean-up of Santa Monica Bay. To my knowledge, the State Water Resources Control Board and the Air Resources Board are separate "agencies." Their respective budgets are considered separately by the legislature. My question: am I prohibited under Government Code sections 87400-87405, or any other relevant law from contracting with the Air Resources Board to act as the facilitator for the Santa Monica Bay Restoration Project? If no conflict exists, are there any restrictions proscribed by the Political Reform Act to such a contract, as I intend to enter into other consulting contracts. Previously you It appears that the image contains a reversed text, likely due to a formatting or scanning issue. The readable content is not fully visible, but some parts can be partially seen and transcribed as follows: "Dear Mr. Breese: To keep Aunt Wendy's taste of the summer alive as I follow: Project. I served as Chairwoman of the Senate Police Reformion posing. My first meeting in September of 1980. We as women in power I am a former member of the State Water Resources Control to keep a disease diagnosis. Now I would rather go into the office day with less fear. If we have a disease diagnosis I would rather suffer complications or have any opinion either on an adults affair or some other mission document. Dear Mr. Breese: Ms. Blanca Breeze April 16, 1990 Page 2 advised me that there was no conflict. Could you kindly provide me with written confirmation of your advice? Thank you. Very truly yours, DANNY WALSH cc: Janann Sharpless The image appears to be a very faint and mostly illegible scan of a document. Only a few scattered words and numbers are partially visible, such as: - "Date 5" - "April 18, 1990" - "Ms. Biswas Bose" - "we will meet on confirmation of hotel specifics. Going on Friday morning" The majority of the text is extremely faint and mostly illegible due to the quality of the scan.
